gdp-real.te_desc: The economy of Estonia expanded 0.7% year-on-year in the final quarter of 2025, easing from a revised 1.1% increase in the previous period. Government spending slowed (3.1% vs 3.4% in Q3), while private consumption remained weak (-0.4%). Net external demand also supported growth, as exports (4.7% vs 4.8%) rose faster than imports (3.1% vs 6.2%). Meanwhile, fixed investments rebounded (6.9% vs -0.5%). industrial-production.te_desc: Industrial production in Estonia fell 3.1% year-on-year in March 2026, after a 0.5% drop in the previous month, marking the lowest level since December 2024. Output declined sharply in electricity, gas, steam, and air conditioning supply (-19.5% vs 4.0% in February), while manufacturing remained in contraction (unchanged at -1.2%), weighed down by food products (-4.1%), leather and related products (-19.5%), paper and paper products (-10.4%), coke and refined petroleum products (-21.8%), basic and pharmaceutical products and preparations (-15.3%), and furniture (-7.6%). inflation-cpi.te_desc: The annual inflation rate in Estonia slowed to 3.4% in April 2026 from 3.6% in the previous month. This marks a 2-month low since February 2026 mainly driven by declines in costs for food and non-alcoholic beverages (2.6% vs 4.2% in March) and healthcare (2.8% vs 11.7%), catering and accommodation (3.1% vs 4.0%). On the other hand, prices increased at a faster pace for transportation (9.2% vs 7%), alcoholic beverages and tobacco (8.3% vs 7.7%), housing (3.7% vs 2.0%), and education and childcare facilities (5.4% vs 0.9%), while clothing and footwear costs declined less (-4.3% vs -5.6%). ppi.te_desc: Producer Prices in Estonia decreased to 159.92 points in March from 165.87 points in February of 2026. Producer Prices in Estonia averaged 96.71 points from 1993 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 166.11 points in January of 2026 and a record low of 26.98 points in January of 1993. source: Statistics Estonia

productivity.te_desc: Productivity in Estonia increased to 118.03 points in the fourth quarter of 2025 from 116.30 points in the third quarter of 2025. Productivity in Estonia averaged 88.47 points from 1995 until 2025, reaching an all time high of 132.84 points in the second quarter of 2021 and a record low of 39.28 points in the first quarter of 1995. source: EUROSTAT

retail-sales.te_desc: Retail sales in Estonia rose by 6.9% year-on-year in March 2026 from 4.8% in the previous month, driven by stronger growth in non-food products excluding fuel, which climbed to 10.1% from 7.9%. Meanwhile, sales of automotive fuel in specialised stores eased to 14.8% from 16.9% in February, while the decline in food, beverages, and tobacco narrowed to 1.8% from 2.6%, with continued weakness attributed to higher food prices. retirement-age-men.te_desc: Retirement Age Men in Estonia remained unchanged at 64.75 Years in 2026 from 64.75 Years in 2025. unemployed-persons.te_desc: The number of unemployed persons in Estonia decreased to 44.32 Thousand in March of 2026 from 46.07 Thousand in February of 2026. Unemployed Persons in Estonia averaged 38.89 Thousand from 1993 until 2026, reaching an all time high of 93.74 Thousand in March of 2010 and a record low of 11.94 Thousand in December of 2006. source: Statistics Estonia

unemployment.te_desc: Estonia’s unemployment rate fell to 7.1% in the first quarter of 2026 from an eleven-year high of 8.6% in the corresponding period last year. The number of unemployed decreased by 12.0 thousand to 52.2 thousand. Of this total, 25.6 thousand were men, compared with 33.7 thousand a year earlier, while 26.7 thousand were women, compared with 30.5 thousand previously. Across age groups, the youth unemployment rate (aged 15–24) rose by 4.0 percentage points to 22.8%, while the rate for those aged 25–49 fell to 6.2% from 8.2%, and for those aged 50–74 declined to 5.2% from 6.9%. unemployment.te_meta_desc: Unemployment Rate in Estonia increased to 7.10 percent in the first quarter of 2026 from 6.40 percent in the fourth quarter of 2025. This page provides - Estonia Unemployment Rate - actual values, historical data, forecast, chart, statistics, economic calendar and news.

youth-unemployment-rate.te_desc: Youth Unemployment Rate in Estonia increased to 21.70 percent in March from 21.60 percent in February of 2026.
